name: Bern's Steak House
description: Bern's Steak House is one of America's most legendary steakhouses, operating since 1956 in South Tampa. Known for its dry-aged steaks, massive wine cellar with over 500,000 bottles, and the famous Harry Waugh Dessert Room upstairs. The wine list is the largest in the world according to Wine Spectator. Reservations are essential and the dress code is business casual.
groupDiningNotes: Bern's can accommodate private dining for groups up to 40 in the wine cellar and private rooms. Pre-fixe menus available for group events. Contact the events team at least 2 weeks in advance for group reservations.

name: Columbia Restaurant
description: Columbia Restaurant is Florida's oldest restaurant and the largest Spanish restaurant in the world, founded in 1905 in Ybor City. Famous for its 1905 Salad prepared tableside, Cuban bread, and traditional paella. The building occupies an entire city block with 15 dining rooms and a courtyard with live flamenco shows nightly. A Tampa landmark for over a century.
groupDiningNotes: Columbia offers private dining rooms for groups of 10 to 300 guests. The Don Quixote room is ideal for mid-size groups. Family-style menus and custom event planning available.

name: Ulele
description: Ulele is housed in a beautifully restored historic water pump building along the Tampa Riverwalk. The menu features Native American-inspired dishes with Florida ingredients, including charbroiled oysters, barbecue shrimp, and alligator hush puppies. The on-site brewery produces craft beers named after Florida history. Outdoor seating overlooks the Hillsborough River.
groupDiningNotes: Ulele has a private dining room for up to 50 guests and can accommodate larger groups on the patio with advance notice. Group menus and buyout options available.

name: Datz
description: Datz is a South Tampa favorite known for creative comfort food, including the Cheesy Todd (a grilled cheese made with glazed donuts) and massive burgers. The restaurant also operates a full bakery and deli with craft beers, artisan cheeses, and house-made desserts. Brunch is a must with inventive cocktails and indulgent plates.
groupDiningNotes: Datz can seat groups of up to 20 with advance reservations. The back dining area works best for larger parties. Contact the restaurant directly for group menus.

name: Mise en Place
description: Mise en Place has been a cornerstone of Tampa's fine dining scene since 1986, led by Chef Marty Blitz. The menu changes weekly, showcasing seasonal ingredients with global influences. Known for inventive tasting menus and an award-winning wine program. The elegant dining room is perfect for special occasions.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining available for groups of 12 to 60 in the upstairs event space. Custom menus and wine pairings can be arranged for group events.

name: Rooster & the Till
description: Rooster & the Till is a beloved Seminole Heights farm-to-table restaurant known for its seasonal, locally sourced menu. The small plates and handmade pastas have earned widespread acclaim. The intimate space has an open kitchen and communal dining feel. Named one of Tampa Bay's best restaurants multiple years running.
groupDiningNotes: The restaurant can accommodate groups of up to 14 at communal tables. Full buyouts available for private events up to 50 guests.

name: Jotoro Kitchen + Taco
description: Jotoro Kitchen + Taco brings elevated Mexican street food to Seminole Heights with house-made tortillas, slow-smoked meats, and creative cocktails. The birria tacos and smoked brisket quesadillas are standout dishes. The casual, vibrant atmosphere makes it a neighborhood favorite for lunch and dinner.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 15 can be seated together. Call ahead for large party reservations. Catering and taco bar options also available.

name: Armature Works
description: Armature Works is a historic streetcar repair facility transformed into a vibrant public market and event space along the Hillsborough River. The Heights Public Market inside features over a dozen food vendors from sushi to BBQ to craft cocktails. The building also hosts events, pop-ups, and has a waterfront bar.
groupDiningNotes: Armature Works is ideal for large groups since everyone can choose their own food from different vendors. Private event spaces available for 50 to 500 guests on the second floor.

name: Sparkman Wharf
description: Sparkman Wharf is a waterfront dining and entertainment district in the Channelside area of downtown Tampa. The outdoor venue features shipping container restaurants with diverse cuisines, a biergarten, and a lawn for live events. Popular options include Ferg's Depot, Jeni's Ice Creams, and Splitsville.
groupDiningNotes: Sparkman Wharf is perfect for groups who want variety. Open-air seating means easy group accommodation. Event spaces and lawn rentals available for private parties.

name: Oxford Exchange
description: Oxford Exchange is a stunning multi-use space in a historic building that combines a restaurant, bookshop, co-working space, and boutique. The restaurant serves elevated brunch and lunch with beautifully plated dishes in a greenhouse-style dining room. The avocado toast and house-made pastries are favorites. A must-visit for Instagram-worthy interiors.
groupDiningNotes: Reservations recommended for groups of 6 or more. Private event space available for up to 200 guests. Brunch service can get busy on weekends.

name: La Segunda Central Bakery
description: La Segunda Central Bakery is the largest producer of authentic Cuban bread in the world, baking over 18,000 loaves daily since 1915. The traditional bread is hand-rolled, palm-frond-topped, and baked in a brick oven. The cafe serves Cuban sandwiches, pastries, and cafe con leche. A Tampa institution and essential stop.
groupDiningNotes: The cafe has limited seating but can accommodate groups of 10 to 15. Pre-orders for large quantities of bread and pastries available for group events.

name: Pia's Trattoria
description: Pia's Trattoria is a cozy Italian restaurant tucked away in the charming village of Gulfport. Featuring house-made pastas, fresh seafood, and a romantic candlelit atmosphere. The outdoor patio is surrounded by tropical plants and string lights. A hidden gem beloved by locals for date nights and celebrations.
groupDiningNotes: Pia's can seat groups of up to 20 in the outdoor courtyard with advance notice. Prix-fixe group menus available for special events.

name: Oystercatchers
description: Oystercatchers at the Grand Hyatt Tampa Bay is a waterfront seafood restaurant overlooking Tampa Bay. Known for its fresh raw bar, Gulf-to-table seafood, and stunning sunset views. The outdoor deck and fire pits create a relaxed upscale atmosphere. Sunday brunch is one of the best in Tampa.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining rooms available for groups of 10 to 80. The waterfront patio can host receptions for up to 150. Customized menus and event planning support provided.

name: Cafe Dufrain
description: Cafe Dufrain is a waterfront restaurant on Harbour Island with views of the Tampa Convention Center and Garrison Channel. Serving American and seafood dishes with Southern influences. The outdoor dock seating is a highlight, and the brunch menu features creative twists on classics.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 30 can be accommodated on the dock patio. Indoor private dining for up to 20. Call ahead for large party arrangements.

name: Ciccio Cali
description: Ciccio Cali brings a California-inspired menu to Tampa's Hyde Park neighborhood with health-conscious and indulgent options alike. Known for its fish tacos, acai bowls, grain bowls, and creative cocktails. The bright, airy space with outdoor patio seating is popular for brunch and happy hour.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 20 welcome with reservations. The covered patio can accommodate larger parties. Group-friendly sharing menus available.

name: Eddie V's Prime Seafood
address: 4400 W Boy Scout Blvd, Tampa, FL 33607
description: Eddie V's Prime Seafood in Tampa offers an upscale dining experience with fresh seafood flown in daily, USDA Prime steaks, and live jazz nightly in the V Lounge. The Chilean sea bass and lobster tail are signature dishes. The refined atmosphere with dark wood and white tablecloths makes it ideal for business dinners.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining rooms available for groups of 12 to 60. Custom menus and wine pairings can be arranged. The V Lounge can be reserved for cocktail receptions.

name: Cooper's Hawk Winery & Restaurants
description: Cooper's Hawk combines a full-service restaurant with a working winery and tasting room. The menu features New American cuisine paired with their house-produced wines. Monthly wine club selections and wine tastings make it a popular destination. The atmosphere is upscale casual with modern decor.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining rooms for groups of 20 to 100. Wine pairing dinners and custom group menus available. The Napa Room is popular for corporate events.

name: Boca Kitchen Bar Market
description: Boca Kitchen Bar Market is a neighborhood restaurant focused on locally sourced ingredients and seasonal menus. The crispy Brussels sprouts, wood-fired pizzas, and house-cured charcuterie are standouts. The attached market sells artisan provisions. Sunday brunch is one of Tampa's best-kept secrets.
groupDiningNotes: Semi-private dining area for groups of up to 25. Custom family-style menus available for parties. Reserve at least a week in advance for groups over 10.

name: Haven
description: Haven is a popular SoHo gastropub with an extensive craft beer selection and creative American fare. The whiskey list features over 200 options and the truffle fries are legendary. Late-night dining and a lively bar scene make it a favorite for Tampa's young professionals.
groupDiningNotes: Haven can seat groups of up to 30 on the patio. Inside booths accommodate 6 to 8 per table. Call ahead for large reservations.

name: Council Oak Steaks & Seafood
description: Council Oak at the Seminole Hard Rock Hotel & Casino Tampa is a premier steakhouse featuring USDA Prime cuts, a raw bar, and signature seafood towers. The sleek, upscale interior complements an extensive wine list. Live piano music adds elegance to the dining experience.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining rooms for groups of 10 to 40. Custom menus and wine pairings available. Valet parking included with Hard Rock validation.

name: Charley's Steak House
description: Charley's Steak House has been serving Tampa since 1972, cooking steaks over a custom wood-burning pit at 1,200 degrees. The bone-in rib eye and filet mignon are perennial favorites. The old-school steakhouse atmosphere features dark wood and leather booths. A Tampa tradition for special occasions.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining for groups of 8 to 50. Prix-fixe group menus available. Ideal for corporate dinners and celebrations.

name: Ella's Americana Folk Art Cafe
description: Ella's is a quirky Seminole Heights cafe filled with folk art, vintage finds, and eclectic decor. The Southern-inspired menu features biscuits and gravy, shrimp and grits, and creative omelets. Known as one of Tampa's best brunch spots with generous portions and a laid-back vibe.
groupDiningNotes: Ella's can seat groups of up to 15 inside. Weekend brunch waits can be long, so arrive early for groups. No reservations accepted.

name: Wright's Gourmet House
address: 1200 S Dale Mabry Hwy, Tampa, FL 33629
description: Wright's Gourmet House has been a Tampa sandwich institution since 1963. Known for over-stuffed deli sandwiches, house-roasted meats, and legendary cakes and desserts made on-site. The lunch line stretches out the door daily, a testament to the quality. The carrot cake and German chocolate cake are local legends.
groupDiningNotes: Wright's has limited indoor seating. For groups over 8, consider ordering ahead for pickup. Catering platters available for group events.

name: Taco Bus
description: Taco Bus started as a converted school bus in 2004 and became a Tampa phenomenon. Serving authentic Mexican street food including tacos al pastor, burritos, gorditas, and fresh salsas around the clock. The original location is still a favorite late-night stop. Multiple locations across Tampa Bay now.
groupDiningNotes: Casual outdoor seating can fit groups of any size. Great option for late-night group dining. No reservations needed.

name: La Teresita
description: La Teresita is a no-frills Cuban restaurant and cafeteria that has served Tampa's West Tampa neighborhood since 1975. Famous for its Cuban sandwich, black beans and rice, roast pork, and cafe con leche. The attached cafeteria side offers quick counter service while the restaurant side has table seating. A working-class Tampa tradition.
groupDiningNotes: The restaurant side can seat groups of up to 25. The cafeteria side is great for quick casual group meals. No reservations taken for the cafeteria.

name: Paddlefish
address: 1482 E Buena Vista Dr, Lake Buena Vista, FL 32830
description: Paddlefish is a modern seafood restaurant aboard a stunning paddleboat-styled vessel at Disney Springs. The rooftop lounge offers spectacular views of the Disney Springs waterfront. Fresh seafood including lobster, crab, and raw bar selections are the focus. The three-story restaurant is a flagship dining experience.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining available on the second level for groups of 15 to 100. The rooftop can be reserved for cocktail receptions. Disney event planning services available.

name: The Ravenous Pig
address: 565 W Fairbanks Ave, Winter Park, FL 32789
description: The Ravenous Pig is a James Beard-nominated gastropub in Winter Park run by husband-and-wife chefs James and Julie Petrakis. Known for its rotating menu of elevated American cuisine with Southern roots and an on-site brewery, Cask & Larder. The pub burger and charcuterie boards are must-tries. One of Central Florida's most acclaimed restaurants.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining room for up to 24 guests. The brewery space can accommodate larger groups. Custom tasting menus available for group events.

name: Hawkers Asian Street Food
description: Hawkers brings the street food stalls of Southeast Asia to Orlando's Mills 50 district. The menu features roti canai, laksa, pad thai, and pork belly bao buns at shareable prices. The original Mills 50 location launched what became a multi-city restaurant group. The vibrant, casual atmosphere buzzes with energy nightly.
groupDiningNotes: Communal tables perfect for groups of 8 to 15. Sharing-style dining makes it ideal for groups. Reservations accepted for parties of 6 or more.

name: Se7en Bites
description: Se7en Bites is a beloved bakery and Southern comfort food restaurant in Orlando's Milk District. Famous for its biscuits, cinnamon rolls the size of your head, and the Biscuit French Toast. Run by Chef Trina Gregory-Propst, the menu celebrates Southern grandma-style cooking. Lines form early on weekends.
groupDiningNotes: Limited seating inside. Groups of 8 or more should plan to arrive early on weekends. Catering and large baked-goods orders available.

name: The Rusty Spoon
description: The Rusty Spoon is a downtown Orlando farm-to-table restaurant by Chef Kathleen Blake. Located near the Dr. Phillips Center, it sources from local farms and purveyors. Known for the Little Farm Burger and seasonal pasta dishes. The rustic-chic dining room with exposed brick is perfect before a show.
groupDiningNotes: Semi-private dining for groups of up to 20. Pre-theater group menus available. Convenient to downtown venues for dinner and a show.
nearbyLandmark: Dr. Phillips Center for the Performing Arts

name: Pig Floyd's Urban Barbakoa
description: Pig Floyd's puts a global twist on barbecue with Korean, Mexican, and Southern influences. Smoked meats are served in creative ways - think brisket egg rolls and smoked pork belly tacos. The casual, counter-service restaurant in Mills 50 has a cult following. Named after Pink Floyd with music-themed decor throughout.
groupDiningNotes: Counter service makes group ordering easy. Indoor and patio seating for groups of up to 20. Catering trays available for larger events.

name: Christner's Prime Steak & Lobster
description: Christner's has been Orlando's go-to special occasion steakhouse since 1993. The intimate, dimly lit dining room serves USDA Prime steaks, 4-pound lobsters, and tableside Caesar salads. The wine list is extensive and the old-school service is impeccable. A true classic steakhouse experience.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining rooms for 10 to 60 guests. Custom menus and wine pairings available. Popular for corporate dinners and rehearsal dinners.

name: Bonefish Grill
address: 7830 W Sand Lake Rd, Orlando, FL 32819
description: Bonefish Grill originated in St. Petersburg and is now a national chain, but the Orlando-area locations remain favorites. Known for wood-grilled fresh fish, Bang Bang Shrimp, and a solid happy hour. The polished casual atmosphere makes it a reliable choice for seafood. The Restaurant Row location is convenient to attractions.
groupDiningNotes: Most locations can accommodate groups of up to 30 with advance notice. Semi-private dining available at select locations.

name: Seasons 52
address: 7700 W Sand Lake Rd, Orlando, FL 32819
description: Seasons 52 is a seasonally inspired restaurant where every dish is under 475 calories, created by the Darden group in Orlando. The menu rotates weekly to feature peak-season ingredients. The mini indulgence desserts are a signature - small, perfectly portioned sweets. The restaurant features live music in the lounge.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining room for groups of 12 to 40. Prix-fixe seasonal menus available for group events. Wine pairing options for each course.

name: Nile Ethiopian Restaurant
description: Nile Ethiopian is one of Central Florida's best Ethiopian restaurants, offering traditional dishes like doro wat, kitfo, and tibs served on injera flatbread. The communal eating style makes it naturally social. Vegetarian platters are generous and flavorful. The warm, welcoming atmosphere celebrates Ethiopian hospitality.
groupDiningNotes: The communal dining style is perfect for groups. Tables for up to 20 available. The vegetarian sampler platter is ideal for sharing among a group.

name: Thai Thani
description: Thai Thani has been serving authentic Thai cuisine on International Drive since 2002. The extensive menu features over 100 dishes including pad thai, green curry, and mango sticky rice. The ornate Thai-themed interior with carved wood and silk creates an immersive dining experience. Consistently rated one of Orlando's best Thai restaurants.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining room for groups of 20 to 50. Family-style group menus available. Vegetarian and allergy-friendly options for diverse groups.

name: Shin Jung Korean Restaurant
description: Shin Jung is a popular Korean restaurant in Orlando's vibrant Mills 50 corridor, known for its Korean BBQ, bibimbap, and kimchi jjigae. The tabletop grills allow guests to cook their own meats, making it a fun interactive experience. Late-night hours and karaoke rooms add to the energy.
groupDiningNotes: Korean BBQ is inherently group-friendly. Private karaoke rooms for 5 to 15 guests. Larger groups can reserve sections of the main dining area.

name: Hanamizuki Japanese Restaurant
description: Hanamizuki is one of Orlando's most authentic Japanese restaurants, popular with Japanese expats and sushi purists. The menu features traditional sashimi, omakase, udon, and ramen. The fish is flown in from Tokyo's Tsukiji Market. The minimalist Japanese interior creates a serene dining experience.
groupDiningNotes: Tatami rooms available for groups of 6 to 12. Omakase group dinners can be arranged with advance notice. Reservations strongly recommended.

name: K Restaurant
description: K Restaurant is a farm-to-table gem in Orlando's College Park neighborhood run by Chef Kevin Fonzo. The menu changes seasonally, emphasizing locally sourced produce and proteins. The intimate dining room and adjacent wine bar offer a refined yet approachable experience. Known for creative tasting menus.
groupDiningNotes: The wine bar side can accommodate groups of up to 12. Full restaurant buyouts available for up to 50 guests. Custom tasting menus for group events.

name: Prato
address: 124 N Park Ave, Winter Park, FL 32789
description: Prato is an upscale Italian restaurant on Park Avenue in Winter Park, featuring wood-fired pizzas, house-made pastas, and seasonal Italian dishes. The lively atmosphere, open kitchen, and stylish decor make it a top date-night spot. The burrata and wood-fired pizza margherita are standouts. Part of the Ravenous Pig restaurant family.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining room for groups of 12 to 30. The bar area can be reserved for cocktail receptions. Group-friendly sharing menus available.

name: The Boheme
description: The Boheme is a refined steakhouse inside the Grand Bohemian Hotel in downtown Orlando. The art-filled dining room showcases rotating gallery exhibitions alongside USDA Prime steaks and fresh seafood. Live jazz and classical music performances add to the sophisticated atmosphere. Sunday brunch is popular.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining for groups of 10 to 40. The Kessler Collection event spaces can accommodate larger groups. Wine dinners and tasting events available.
nearbyLandmark: Dr. Phillips Center for the Performing Arts

name: Victoria & Albert's
description: Victoria & Albert's at Disney's Grand Floridian Resort is Central Florida's only AAA Five Diamond restaurant. The prix-fixe tasting menu features 7 to 10 courses of world-class cuisine with wine pairings. The intimate dining room seats just 56 guests. Chef's Table and Queen Victoria's Room offer exclusive experiences. Jackets required for gentlemen.
groupDiningNotes: Queen Victoria's Room accommodates up to 8 guests for a private experience. The main dining room is not suitable for large groups. Reserve months in advance.

name: California Grill
description: California Grill sits atop Disney's Contemporary Resort on the 15th floor with panoramic views of Magic Kingdom and the nightly fireworks. The menu features Pacific Rim-inspired cuisine with sushi, seasonal entrees, and a famous Sunday brunch. Guests can step onto the observation deck during fireworks. One of Disney's most popular dining reservations.
groupDiningNotes: Semi-private dining for groups of up to 16. Full restaurant buyouts available for special events. Reserve 180 days in advance for groups.

name: Jiko - The Cooking Place
description: Jiko at Disney's Animal Kingdom Lodge features African and Indian-inspired cuisine cooked over oak and African hardwoods. The wine list exclusively features South African wines. Signature dishes include the filet mignon with maize grits and the oak-grilled fish. The warm, elegant dining room overlooks a savanna.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining in the Cape Town wine room for groups of 8 to 14. Custom menus and South African wine pairings available. A unique group dining experience.

name: Takumi-Tei
description: Takumi-Tei in EPCOT's Japan Pavilion is a multi-course Japanese fine dining experience inspired by five different elements of Japanese culture. The interior features five distinct themed rooms with authentic Japanese design. Wagyu beef, sashimi, and kaiseki-style courses are highlights. One of the most exclusive dining experiences at Walt Disney World.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining rooms for groups of 4 to 8. Omakase experiences can be customized. Reservations required well in advance.

name: Space 220
description: Space 220 at EPCOT simulates dining 220 miles above Earth in a space station. Guests ride a space elevator to the restaurant where digital windows show Earth below. The prix-fixe menu features modern American cuisine with space-themed presentations. One of Disney's most unique and sought-after dining experiences.
groupDiningNotes: Space 220 can accommodate groups of up to 10 with advance reservations. The lounge offers a la carte options for smaller groups. Book as early as possible as reservations are extremely competitive.

name: Mythos Restaurant
description: Mythos at Universal's Islands of Adventure has been voted the world's best theme park restaurant multiple times by Theme Park Insider. Set in a stunning cave-like structure overlooking the lagoon in The Lost Continent area. The menu features Mediterranean-inspired dishes, craft cocktails, and creative desserts. Park admission required.
groupDiningNotes: Mythos can seat groups of up to 20 with advance reservations. Waterfront patio available for larger parties. Contact Universal group dining services for events.

name: The Kitchen by Wolfgang Puck
description: The Kitchen at Universal CityWalk is Wolfgang Puck's casual-chic restaurant featuring pizzas, pastas, and American comfort food. The open kitchen and lively atmosphere make it great for families and groups. Signature dishes include the bacon-wrapped meatloaf and seasonal flatbreads. No park admission required.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 25 can be accommodated with reservations. Universal group dining coordination available. Great option for groups visiting the parks.

name: Bubba Gump Shrimp Co.
description: Bubba Gump Shrimp Co. on International Drive is a Forrest Gump-themed restaurant serving Southern-style seafood. The menu features shrimp prepared in every way imaginable plus burgers, ribs, and salads. The fun, family-friendly atmosphere includes movie trivia and memorabilia throughout. A crowd-pleaser for groups with kids.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 40 can be seated together. Party rooms and group menus available. Fun for team outings and family reunions.

name: Cafe Tu Tu Tango
description: Cafe Tu Tu Tango is an artist-themed restaurant on International Drive where live artists paint while you dine. The tapas-style menu spans global cuisines from empanadas to pizza to stir-fry. The bohemian atmosphere includes live performances and rotating art shows. A unique Orlando dining experience for over 30 years.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 50 can be accommodated. The sharing-style menu is ideal for groups. Private event space available upstairs.

name: The Capital Grille
description: The Capital Grille at Pointe Orlando is an upscale steakhouse known for dry-aged steaks, fresh seafood, and an award-winning wine list. The Kona-crusted sirloin and lobster mac and cheese are signature dishes. The dark wood and leather interior creates a classic steakhouse ambiance ideal for business and celebrations.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining rooms for 10 to 40 guests. Custom menus, wine pairings, and A/V equipment available for corporate events. Valet parking at Pointe Orlando.

name: Ruth's Chris Steak House
address: 7501 W Sand Lake Rd, Orlando, FL 32819
description: Ruth's Chris on Restaurant Row serves USDA Prime steaks sizzling on 500-degree plates - the chain's signature presentation. The creamed spinach and potatoes au gratin are classic sides. The refined dining room with white tablecloths is a top choice for special occasions. Consistent quality has made it an Orlando staple for decades.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining rooms for groups of 12 to 60. Custom menus and wine pairings available. The Board Room seats up to 14 for intimate gatherings.

name: The Palm
description: The Palm at Universal's Hard Rock Hotel is a classic New York-style steakhouse with caricature-lined walls. Serving prime steaks, jumbo lobsters, and Italian classics since the original opened in 1926. The Orlando location brings the same timeless atmosphere near Universal Studios. A favorite for power lunches and celebratory dinners.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining for groups of 10 to 50. Custom menus available. Convenient for groups visiting Universal parks.

name: Hot N Juicy Crawfish
address: 7572 W Sand Lake Rd, Orlando, FL 32819
description: Hot N Juicy Crawfish serves hands-on Cajun seafood boils in plastic bags with your choice of seasoning and spice level. Crawfish, shrimp, crab, and lobster are the stars. Bibs and gloves are provided for the messy, fun eating experience. A lively spot that captures the spirit of a Louisiana crawfish boil.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 25 can be seated together at long tables. The communal, hands-on dining style is naturally group-friendly. No reservations - first come, first served.

name: 4 Rivers Smokehouse
address: 2103 W Fairbanks Ave, Winter Park, FL 32789
description: 4 Rivers Smokehouse started in Winter Park and grew into one of Central Florida's most beloved BBQ chains. The 18-hour smoked brisket, burnt ends, and signature Sweet Shop desserts draw lines daily. Founded by John Rivers, the restaurant also supports local charities through its community-focused mission.
groupDiningNotes: Counter service makes group ordering simple. Catering packages from small groups to hundreds. Family packs and meat-by-the-pound options available.

name: Torchy's Tacos
description: Torchy's Tacos brings Austin, Texas-style tacos to Orlando with creative options like the Trailer Park (fried chicken, green chiles, queso) and the Democrat (barbacoa, avocado, queso). Known for the Green Chile Queso and hand-shaken margaritas. The fun, counter-service format is quick and casual with bold flavors.
groupDiningNotes: Counter service with ample seating. Groups of any size welcome. Catering and taco bar packages available for events.

name: Keke's Breakfast Cafe
description: Keke's Breakfast Cafe is a Central Florida institution serving breakfast and lunch daily with locations throughout the Orlando area. Known for made-to-order omelets, fluffy pancakes, and fresh fruit waffles. The menu features over 50 options including healthy choices. Open 7 AM to 2:30 PM daily - a breakfast lover's paradise.
groupDiningNotes: Most locations can seat groups of up to 15. Arrive early on weekends to avoid long waits. No reservations accepted - first come, first served.

name: First Watch
description: First Watch is a daytime dining concept serving breakfast, brunch, and lunch with fresh, made-to-order dishes. Originally from Florida, the chain has grown nationally but maintains quality. Known for the Million Dollar Bacon, avocado toast, and seasonal specials. Juices are pressed fresh daily in-house.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 12 can be seated together at most locations. The Audubon Park location has a great patio. No dinner service - open until 2:30 PM.

name: The Polite Pig
address: 1536 E Buena Vista Dr, Lake Buena Vista, FL 32830
description: The Polite Pig at Disney Springs is a Southern BBQ restaurant from the team behind 4 Rivers Smokehouse. Featuring wood-fired, smoked-then-grilled meats, house-made sides, and craft cocktails on tap. The smoked chicken wings and Southern Slide pork sandwich are favorites. Modern counter-service with indoor and patio seating.
groupDiningNotes: Counter service makes it easy for groups. Patio seating accommodates large parties. One of the more affordable Disney Springs options for groups.

name: Morimoto Asia
address: 1600 E Buena Vista Dr, Lake Buena Vista, FL 32830
description: Morimoto Asia at Disney Springs is Iron Chef Masaharu Morimoto's pan-Asian restaurant featuring Chinese, Japanese, and Korean cuisines. The dramatic two-story dining room with an exhibition kitchen showcases Peking duck, sushi, and dim sum. The Forbidden Lounge upstairs serves craft cocktails with DJ entertainment on weekends.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining for groups of 12 to 40 in the upstairs lounge. Full restaurant buyouts available. Group dim sum and family-style menus can be arranged.

name: Jaleo by Jose Andres
address: 1580 E Buena Vista Dr, Lake Buena Vista, FL 32830
description: Jaleo at Disney Springs is celebrity chef Jose Andres' Spanish tapas restaurant featuring traditional and avant-garde Spanish cuisine. The menu includes classic paella, jamon Iberico, and the famous Tortilla Espanola. The colorful, energetic atmosphere and open kitchen create a festive dining experience. One of Disney Springs' most acclaimed restaurants.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining for groups of 20 to 60. Family-style tapas menus ideal for sharing among groups. Sangria and cocktail packages available.

name: Wine Bar George
address: 1610 E Buena Vista Dr, Lake Buena Vista, FL 32830
description: Wine Bar George at Disney Springs is Master Sommelier George Miliotes' wine bar featuring over 100 wines by the glass and ounce. The food menu of cheese boards, flatbreads, and small plates is designed to complement the wine selections. The Brunch à la George includes bottomless sparkling wine. A sophisticated yet approachable wine experience.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 20 can be accommodated. Wine education tastings available for groups. The rooftop terrace is ideal for cocktail parties.

name: STK Orlando
address: 1580 E Buena Vista Dr, Lake Buena Vista, FL 32830
description: STK Orlando at Disney Springs combines a modern steakhouse with a chic lounge atmosphere, complete with a live DJ. The menu features USDA Prime cuts, seafood, and signature cocktails. The rooftop terrace offers stunning views of Disney Springs. A trendy spot that blurs the line between restaurant and nightlife.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining and VIP sections for groups of 10 to 100. The rooftop terrace can be reserved for private events. DJ and entertainment packages available.

name: The BOATHOUSE
address: 1620 E Buena Vista Dr, Lake Buena Vista, FL 32830
description: The BOATHOUSE at Disney Springs is a waterfront restaurant featuring steaks, chops, and fresh seafood with a nautical theme. The restaurant showcases a collection of dream boats and amphicars that take guests on guided tours of the waterway. The raw bar and lobster bake are standout menu items. Live music on the waterfront dock.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining for groups of 15 to 50. The Captain's Table seats up to 12 for intimate gatherings. Amphicar rides can be booked for group fun.

name: Toothsome Chocolate Emporium & Savory Feast Kitchen
description: Toothsome Chocolate Emporium at Universal CityWalk is a steampunk-themed restaurant featuring towering milkshakes, artisan chocolates, and a full savory menu. The all-day brunch, gourmet burgers, and pasta dishes are excellent, but the show-stopping desserts are the main draw. The elaborate steampunk decor and roaming characters add to the spectacle.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 20 can be seated together. The dessert-sharing format is great for groups. No park admission required.

name: The Fat Snook
address: 2464 S Atlantic Ave, Cocoa Beach, FL 32931
description: The Fat Snook is a critically acclaimed restaurant in Cocoa Beach known for its fresh, Caribbean-influenced seafood and creative presentations. The intimate BYOB restaurant features a seasonal menu with locally caught fish and house-made desserts. Named one of Florida's best restaurants by Florida Trend. Reservations strongly recommended.
groupDiningNotes: The intimate space seats about 40 total. Groups of up to 10 can be accommodated with advance reservations. BYOB allows groups to bring their own wine.

name: Grills Seafood Deck & Tiki Bar
address: 505 Glen Cheek Dr, Cape Canaveral, FL 32920
description: Grills Seafood at Port Canaveral is a waterfront seafood restaurant overlooking the port where cruise ships depart. Fresh catches include grouper, mahi, and stone crab in season. The tiki bar and outdoor deck offer live music and views of rocket launches from nearby Kennedy Space Center. A must-stop before or after a cruise.
groupDiningNotes: The spacious deck can accommodate groups of 30 or more. Private event space upstairs for up to 100. Perfect for pre-cruise group meals.

name: Fishlips Waterfront Bar & Grill
address: 610 Glen Cheek Dr, Cape Canaveral, FL 32920
description: Fishlips sits on the water at Port Canaveral with views of cruise ships, fishing boats, and the occasional rocket launch. The menu features fresh-off-the-boat seafood including blackened grouper, peel-and-eat shrimp, and fish tacos. The casual waterfront atmosphere and live music make it a local favorite. Great for sunset dining.
groupDiningNotes: Waterfront deck accommodates groups of up to 25. Indoor dining for larger parties. Group-friendly menu with shareable appetizers.

name: Rusty's Seafood & Oyster Bar
address: 628 Glen Cheek Dr, Cape Canaveral, FL 32920
description: Rusty's Seafood at Port Canaveral is a casual waterfront spot famous for its raw bar, steamed seafood, and cold beer. Overlooking the port with views of departing cruise ships and returning fishing boats. The oysters, clam chowder, and bucket of steamed shrimp are popular choices. Open late with live music on weekends.
groupDiningNotes: Bar and deck seating for groups of up to 20. The casual atmosphere is great for informal group gatherings. Happy hour deals on raw bar items.

name: Coconuts on the Beach
description: Coconuts on the Beach is the quintessential Florida beach bar right on the sand in Cocoa Beach. Serving fresh seafood, burgers, and tropical cocktails with your toes in the sand. Live music nightly draws locals and tourists alike. The casual, flip-flop-friendly atmosphere epitomizes the Space Coast beach lifestyle.
groupDiningNotes: Beach-side seating for groups of any size. The open-air format accommodates large parties easily. Live music creates a festive group atmosphere.

name: Jazzy's Mainely Lobster
address: 206 N Orlando Ave, Cocoa Beach, FL 32931
description: Jazzy's Mainely Lobster brings New England-style lobster rolls and seafood to the Space Coast. The lobster rolls come Maine-style (cold with mayo) or Connecticut-style (warm with butter). Clam chowder, lobster bisque, and fried clams round out the menu. A small, casual spot that punches above its weight.
groupDiningNotes: Small restaurant with limited seating. Groups of 6 to 8 can be accommodated. Takeout is a great option for larger groups heading to the beach.

name: Long Doggers
address: 3050 N Atlantic Ave, Cocoa Beach, FL 32931
description: Long Doggers is a surf-themed restaurant and bar chain originating on the Space Coast. Known for burgers, wings, and a laid-back surfer vibe. The menu features creative hot dogs, fresh salads, and cold beer. Multiple locations across Brevard County with each having its own local character.
groupDiningNotes: Casual atmosphere welcomes groups of any size. Great for large group casual dining after beach activities. Easy parking and walkable from the beach.

name: Sunset Cafe
address: 2 S Atlantic Ave, Cocoa Beach, FL 32931
description: Sunset Cafe is a charming Cocoa Beach breakfast and lunch spot beloved by locals. Known for creative omelets, fresh-squeezed juices, and generous portions at affordable prices. The cozy, no-frills atmosphere focuses on good food rather than fancy decor. Opens early for surfers and beachgoers.
groupDiningNotes: Small restaurant - groups of 6 to 8 can be accommodated. Arrive early to avoid waits, especially on weekends. Cash preferred.

name: Roberto's Little Havana
description: Roberto's Little Havana brings authentic Cuban cuisine to downtown Melbourne with family recipes passed through generations. The Cuban sandwich, ropa vieja, and black beans are standout dishes. The small, colorful restaurant has a loyal local following. Live Latin music on select nights adds to the atmosphere.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 12 can be seated together. The intimate space fills quickly, so call ahead. Catering for larger events available.

name: Crush XI
description: Crush XI is an upscale wine bar and restaurant in Melbourne's Eau Gallie Arts District. The seasonal menu pairs creative small plates and entrees with an extensive wine list. Charcuterie boards, lamb chops, and duck breast are popular. The intimate, dimly lit space is perfect for date nights on the Space Coast.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining for groups of up to 16. Wine tasting events and pairing dinners available. Reserve well in advance for groups.

name: Matt's Casbah
address: 801 E New Haven Ave, Melbourne, FL 32901
description: Matt's Casbah is a popular Melbourne restaurant serving globally-inspired cuisine with Mediterranean, Asian, and Southern influences. The tropical courtyard, live music, and eclectic decor create a vibrant atmosphere. Known for the lobster mac and cheese and creative cocktails. A downtown Melbourne anchor since 2007.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 30 on the outdoor patio. Private event space for up to 60. Live music nights are great for group celebrations.

name: Hemingway's Tavern
address: 725 E New Haven Ave, Melbourne, FL 32901
description: Hemingway's Tavern is a literary-themed gastropub in downtown Melbourne featuring craft beers, creative cocktails, and elevated pub fare. The menu includes gourmet burgers, flatbreads, and seasonal specials. The cozy, book-filled atmosphere with exposed brick is a gathering spot for locals.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 20 can be accommodated inside. The bar area is great for casual group gatherings. Trivia nights and live music are fun group activities.

name: Slow & Low BBQ
address: 306 N Orlando Ave, Cocoa Beach, FL 32931
description: Slow & Low BBQ is a beloved Cocoa Beach barbecue joint smoking meats low and slow in-house daily. The pulled pork, brisket, and baby back ribs draw rave reviews from locals and visitors alike. Homemade sides include mac and cheese, baked beans, and coleslaw. Casual counter service with indoor and outdoor seating.
groupDiningNotes: Counter service with ample seating. Groups of any size welcome. Meat-by-the-pound and family pack options perfect for group orders.

name: Juice 'N Java
address: 4 N Atlantic Ave, Cocoa Beach, FL 32931
description: Juice 'N Java is a Cocoa Beach staple serving freshly squeezed juices, smoothies, espresso drinks, and a full breakfast and lunch menu. Located steps from the beach, it is a popular morning stop for surfers and beachgoers. The acai bowls and breakfast wraps fuel active lifestyles. Open air seating with ocean breezes.
groupDiningNotes: Casual cafe seating for groups of up to 10. Counter service is quick and easy for groups. Perfect pre-beach fueling spot.

name: Florida's Seafood Bar & Grill
address: 480 Glen Cheek Dr, Cape Canaveral, FL 32920
description: Florida's Seafood at Port Canaveral is a family-owned waterfront restaurant serving fresh Florida seafood for over 30 years. Known for their rock shrimp, grouper sandwiches, and affordable lunch specials. The casual, no-pretense atmosphere focuses on great seafood at fair prices. Outdoor deck overlooks the port.
groupDiningNotes: Deck seating for groups of up to 20. Indoor dining for larger parties with advance notice. Affordable group-friendly pricing.

name: Brano Italian Restaurant
address: 301 W Cocoa Beach Cswy, Cocoa Beach, FL 32931
description: Brano Italian brings authentic Italian cuisine to Cocoa Beach with hand-made pastas, wood-fired pizzas, and imported Italian ingredients. The cozy restaurant features a warm, rustic Italian atmosphere. Chicken parmigiana, osso buco, and tiramisu are favorites. A consistent neighborhood favorite for Italian food on the Space Coast.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 16 can be seated with reservations. Family-style dining options available for groups. The intimate setting is ideal for celebrations.

name: Simply Delicious
description: Simply Delicious is a popular Titusville cafe and bakery serving breakfast, lunch, and fresh-baked goods. Known for quiches, sandwiches on house-made bread, and a dessert case full of cakes and pastries. The charming, homey atmosphere makes it a local gathering spot. A great stop before heading to Kennedy Space Center.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 10 can be seated together. Pre-orders recommended for large pastry and catering orders. Close to Kennedy Space Center Visitor Complex.

name: Playalinda Brewing Company
description: Playalinda Brewing is a craft brewery in downtown Titusville named after the nearby Playalinda Beach in the Canaveral National Seashore. The taproom features rotating craft beers, wood-fired pizzas, and a fun, space-coast vibe with rocket-themed beer names. The Hardware Store location in a restored 1920s building adds character.
groupDiningNotes: Brewery taproom accommodates groups of 20 or more. Private event space available. Brewery tours for groups can be arranged.

name: SunSpot Bar & Grill
address: 6555 N Atlantic Ave, Cape Canaveral, FL 32920
description: SunSpot is a laid-back beach bar and grill in Cape Canaveral serving burgers, wings, and cold drinks. The casual, open-air atmosphere is popular with locals and beach visitors. Live music on weekends and a pool table inside add to the fun. A solid neighborhood bar with good food at fair prices.
groupDiningNotes: Open-air bar accommodates groups easily. Great for casual group hangouts. No reservations needed.

name: Casa D'Angelo Ristorante
address: 1201 N Federal Hwy, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33304
description: Casa D'Angelo is one of South Florida's most acclaimed Italian restaurants, led by Chef Angelo Elia. The house-made pastas, osso buco, and fresh seafood rival the best Italian restaurants in the country. The warm, elegant dining room features Tuscan-inspired decor. A Fort Lauderdale institution for over 20 years.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining room for groups of 12 to 30. Custom Italian menus and wine pairings available. Popular for rehearsal dinners and corporate events.

name: Timpano Italian Chophouse
address: 450 E Las Olas Blvd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33301
description: Timpano Italian Chophouse on Las Olas Boulevard combines classic Italian cuisine with premium steaks in a glamorous 1940s supper club setting. Live music, craft cocktails, and an extensive wine list complement the menu. The veal chop and lobster ravioli are signature dishes. The stunning interior transports guests to old Hollywood.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining for groups of 15 to 80. The live music atmosphere enhances group celebrations. Custom event planning and AV equipment available.

name: Shooters Waterfront
description: Shooters Waterfront is a Fort Lauderdale landmark on the Intracoastal Waterway, famous for yacht-watching, fresh seafood, and a vibrant bar scene. The waterfront patio is one of South Florida's most iconic dining spots. The menu features seafood, steaks, and creative cocktails. A see-and-be-seen destination since the 1980s.
groupDiningNotes: Waterfront patio accommodates groups of 30 or more. Private event space for up to 200. Dock your boat and dine - boat slips available.

name: 15th Street Fisheries
description: 15th Street Fisheries is a waterfront seafood institution at Lauderdale Marina, operating since 1979. Known for fresh catches, the famous Mahi sandwich, and tarpon feeding from the dock. The upstairs dining room offers white-tablecloth service while the dockside bar is more casual. Views of mega-yachts and the Intracoastal.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining upstairs for groups of 20 to 100. Dockside bar area for casual group gatherings. Boat-accessible with marina docking available.

name: Rustic Inn Crabhouse
description: Rustic Inn Crabhouse has been a Fort Lauderdale institution since 1955, famous for its garlic crabs served on paper-covered tables with wooden mallets. The no-frills, hands-on dining experience is messy and fun. The waterfront location on the New River adds charm. A true old-Florida seafood experience.
groupDiningNotes: Large group seating on the waterfront deck for 30 or more. The communal, hands-on dining style is perfect for groups. No reservations needed - first come, first served.

name: Lester's Diner
description: Lester's Diner is a classic 24-hour diner that has been serving Fort Lauderdale since 1968. Known for enormous portions, homemade baked goods, and the famous giant layer cakes displayed in the front case. Breakfast is served all day and the meatloaf is a customer favorite. A true neighborhood institution.
groupDiningNotes: Large booths and tables accommodate groups of up to 15. Open 24 hours makes it convenient for any schedule. No reservations - walk-in only.

name: Louie Bossi's Ristorante Bar Pizzeria
address: 100 E Las Olas Blvd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33301
description: Louie Bossi's on Las Olas is one of Fort Lauderdale's most popular Italian restaurants, featuring wood-fired pizzas, house-made pastas, and an extensive Italian wine list. The bustling atmosphere, outdoor patio, and stylish decor make it a scene. Known for the burrata, carbonara, and creative cocktails.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 20 on the patio or inside. Private dining available for larger events. Reservations strongly recommended, especially weekends.

name: American Social
address: 721 E Las Olas Blvd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33301
description: American Social on Las Olas is an upscale sports bar with waterfront views along the New River. Dozens of TVs, craft beers, and an elevated bar food menu featuring gourmet burgers, truffle fries, and sushi rolls. The outdoor patio on the river is a prime spot for watching games and sunsets.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 40 on the riverfront patio. Private events for up to 100. Great for watch parties and team outings.

name: Coconuts by the Fort Lauderdale Beach
description: Coconuts is a waterfront restaurant on the Intracoastal at Fort Lauderdale Beach serving seafood, burgers, and tropical drinks. The open-air deck offers views of boats, water taxis, and the famous drawbridge. A relaxed, quintessentially Fort Lauderdale experience with live music and a friendly bar scene.
groupDiningNotes: Waterfront deck seats groups of 20 or more. Casual atmosphere is great for informal gatherings. Boat and water taxi accessible.

name: S3 Sun Surf Sand
address: 301 S Fort Lauderdale Beach Blvd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33316
description: S3 Sun Surf Sand is a beachfront restaurant at the Hilton Fort Lauderdale Beach Resort offering ocean views and a modern seafood-focused menu. Sushi, ceviche, and grilled seafood pair with craft cocktails. The sleek rooftop setting and direct beach access make it a premium beachside dining experience.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 20 on the terrace. Private events for up to 80. Beachfront views enhance any group celebration.

name: Burlock Coast
address: 1 N Fort Lauderdale Beach Blvd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33304
description: Burlock Coast at The Ritz-Carlton Fort Lauderdale is a Prohibition-era-themed restaurant featuring craft cocktails, a rum bar, and coastal cuisine. Named after rum-runners of the 1920s, the menu highlights fresh seafood and artisan ingredients. The oceanfront patio and stylish interior make it one of the area's most atmospheric restaurants.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining for groups of 12 to 40. The oceanfront terrace can host receptions. Ritz-Carlton event services available for larger functions.

name: Wild Sea Oyster Bar & Grille
address: 620 E Las Olas Blvd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33301
description: Wild Sea is an upscale seafood restaurant on Las Olas Boulevard by the Riverside Hotel. The raw bar features East and West Coast oysters, and the menu showcases whole-roasted fish and premium seafood towers. The New England-meets-South Florida aesthetic creates a polished nautical atmosphere.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining for groups of 10 to 30. The raw bar and seafood towers are great for group sharing. Riverside Hotel event coordination available.

name: Mastro's Ocean Club
address: 1 N Fort Lauderdale Beach Blvd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33304
description: Mastro's Ocean Club in Fort Lauderdale is an ultra-premium steakhouse and seafood restaurant known for its warm butter cake dessert and seafood towers. The upscale oceanfront setting, live entertainment, and impeccable service create a special-occasion atmosphere. One of the most expensive and celebrated restaurants in South Florida.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining for groups of 10 to 50. VIP treatment and custom menus available. Valet parking included. Reserve well in advance.

name: Il Mulino New York
address: 1800 E Sunrise Blvd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33304
description: Il Mulino New York brings the acclaimed Manhattan Italian restaurant experience to Fort Lauderdale. The classic Italian menu features veal, seafood, and house-made pastas in generous portions. The complimentary appetizer spread at the start of each meal is legendary. White tablecloths and professional service define the experience.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining for groups of 12 to 40. Custom menus and wine pairings available. One of the top Italian restaurants in South Florida.

name: El Camino
description: El Camino is a lively Fort Lauderdale Mexican restaurant known for its tacos, tequila, and vibrant atmosphere. The extensive tequila and mezcal menu pairs with street-style tacos and fresh guacamole. The colorful, retro interior and outdoor patio draw a fun crowd. Happy hour is one of the best on Las Olas.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 30 on the patio. Tequila tasting packages for groups. The festive atmosphere is ideal for celebrations.

name: Quarterdeck Seafood Bar
description: Quarterdeck is a casual seafood bar chain born in Fort Lauderdale, known for steamed seafood, cold beer, and a no-frills atmosphere. The steamed clams, peel-and-eat shrimp, and raw bar are popular among locals. Multiple locations across South Florida make it a reliable neighborhood seafood option at affordable prices.
groupDiningNotes: Casual seating for groups of any size. The communal, relaxed atmosphere is naturally group-friendly. No reservations needed.

name: Henry's Sandwich Station
description: Henry's Sandwich Station is a beloved Fort Lauderdale sandwich shop serving creative gourmet sandwiches and loaded fries. The Porky Pig, chicken parm hero, and truffle fries have a cult following. The small, casual shop focuses on quality ingredients and big flavors in every bite. A Yelp favorite with consistently high ratings.
groupDiningNotes: Small shop with limited seating. Groups of 4 to 6 can eat in. Takeout recommended for larger groups. Catering trays available.

name: Luigi's Coal Oven Pizza
address: 1415 E Las Olas Blvd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33301
description: Luigi's Coal Oven Pizza serves coal-fired thin-crust pizzas and Italian favorites on Las Olas. The 900-degree coal oven produces a perfectly charred, crispy crust. Pastas, salads, and Italian entrees round out the menu. The casual, bustling atmosphere on Las Olas makes it a local favorite for quick Italian dining.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 15 can be seated together. Pizza sharing makes it ideal for groups. Multiple locations in Fort Lauderdale.

name: Casablanca Cafe
description: Casablanca Cafe is housed in a historic 1920s Mediterranean-style building right on Fort Lauderdale Beach. The oceanfront patio is one of the most scenic dining spots in South Florida. The menu features seafood, steaks, and Mediterranean dishes. Live music, ocean breezes, and sunset views make it an unforgettable dining experience.
groupDiningNotes: Oceanfront patio for groups of up to 30. Private event space for up to 80. One of the most scenic group dining venues in Fort Lauderdale.

name: The Cellar
description: The Cellar is Daytona Beach's premier fine dining restaurant located in a historic building downtown. The Italian and continental menu features veal, fresh seafood, and hand-made pastas in an intimate, candlelit setting. The wine cellar houses an impressive collection. A Daytona institution for special occasions since 1993.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining room for groups of 10 to 30. Custom menus and wine pairings available. Ideal for rehearsal dinners and celebrations.

name: Aunt Catfish's on the River
description: Aunt Catfish's is a beloved Southern seafood restaurant on the Intracoastal Waterway in Port Orange, just south of Daytona. Famous for its all-you-can-eat catfish, cinnamon rolls, and waterfront dining. Sunday brunch draws crowds from across Volusia County. The dock and tiki bar add to the old-Florida charm.
groupDiningNotes: Large parties welcome with advance reservations. The waterfront patio and dock accommodate groups of 30 or more. All-you-can-eat option simplifies group ordering.

name: Down the Hatch
description: Down the Hatch is a waterfront seafood restaurant at the Halifax Harbor Marina in Ponce Inlet. Fresh catches, fried seafood baskets, and cold drinks are served with views of fishing boats and the Ponce de Leon Inlet Lighthouse. Dolphins regularly swim by the dock. A quintessential old-Florida seafood experience.
groupDiningNotes: Waterfront deck for groups of 20 or more. Casual dock-side seating is first come, first served. Perfect for casual group outings near the inlet.

name: Grind Gastropub & Kafe
address: 49 W International Speedway Blvd, Daytona Beach, FL 32114
description: Grind Gastropub is a downtown Daytona gastropub with a chef-driven menu of elevated comfort food, craft cocktails, and local beers. Gourmet burgers, mac and cheese, and creative daily specials are highlights. The industrial-chic space with exposed brick doubles as a coffee shop by morning. A revitalizing force in downtown Daytona.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 20 can be accommodated. The casual atmosphere is great for group outings. Close to the Speedway and beach.

name: Rose Villa Restaurant
address: 43 W Granada Blvd, Ormond Beach, FL 32174
description: Rose Villa is housed in a beautifully restored 1901 Queen Anne Victorian home in Ormond Beach. The intimate fine dining experience features continental cuisine with seasonal menus, fresh seafood, and an extensive wine list. Each dining room in the house offers a unique ambiance. A romantic destination for celebrations.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining rooms for groups of 8 to 20. The parlor and garden rooms can be reserved. Custom menus available for special events.

name: Hyde Park Prime Steakhouse
address: 2637 S Atlantic Ave, Daytona Beach Shores, FL 32118
description: Hyde Park Prime Steakhouse offers a premium steakhouse experience on the Daytona Beach oceanfront. USDA Prime steaks, fresh seafood, and an award-winning wine list are highlights. The ocean view dining room creates an upscale atmosphere for business and celebrations. One of the best steakhouses on Florida's east coast.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining for groups of 10 to 40. Ocean-view seating for group events. Custom menus and wine pairings available.

name: Crabby's Oceanside
address: 3500 Ocean Shore Blvd, Ormond Beach, FL 32176
description: Crabby's Oceanside is an open-air beachfront restaurant overlooking the Atlantic in Ormond Beach. Fresh seafood, tropical cocktails, and live music create a classic beach bar atmosphere. The crab legs, coconut shrimp, and frozen drinks are favorites. One of the best spots to watch the sunrise on the east coast.
groupDiningNotes: Open-air deck accommodates groups of any size. The casual beachfront setting is perfect for large gatherings. No reservations needed.

name: Racing's North Turn Beach Bar & Grille
address: 4511 S Atlantic Ave, Ponce Inlet, FL 32127
description: Racing's North Turn sits on the exact stretch of Daytona Beach where NASCAR was born - the original north turn of the beach/road course. Racing memorabilia fills the restaurant while the outdoor deck overlooks the beach. Burgers, seafood, and cold beer pay homage to Daytona's racing heritage. A must-visit for motorsports fans.
groupDiningNotes: Beachfront deck for groups of 20 or more. The racing history makes it a unique group outing. Close to Ponce Inlet attractions.

name: The Garlic
address: 556 E Third Ave, New Smyrna Beach, FL 32169
description: The Garlic in New Smyrna Beach is a neighborhood favorite known for its garlic-centric menu featuring seafood, pasta, and steaks. The garlic bread, shrimp scampi, and garlic-crusted grouper are signatures. The casual, friendly atmosphere and consistent quality have made it a Volusia County institution for over 20 years.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 15 can be accommodated. The garlic-themed menu is a fun group dining experience. Reservations recommended on weekends.

name: Hog Heaven BBQ
description: Hog Heaven is a roadside BBQ joint in Edgewater near Daytona Beach, smoking meats low and slow over oak and hickory. The pulled pork, baby back ribs, and smoked chicken draw locals from across Volusia County. The small, no-frills setup focuses entirely on the food. Cash only and closes when the meat runs out.
groupDiningNotes: Limited seating at picnic tables. Groups should call ahead for large orders. Perfect for picking up BBQ for a group gathering elsewhere.

name: Ronin Sushi & Bar
address: 111 W International Speedway Blvd, Daytona Beach, FL 32114
description: Ronin Sushi & Bar is a modern Japanese restaurant and sushi bar in downtown Daytona Beach. Creative sushi rolls, ramen, and Japanese-inspired cocktails are served in a sleek, contemporary setting. The omakase option lets the chef showcase the freshest fish. A welcome addition to downtown Daytona's growing dining scene.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 15 at communal tables. The sharing-style menu works well for groups. Sake and cocktail packages available.

name: Tia Cori's Tacos
description: Tia Cori's Tacos is a colorful downtown Daytona taco shop known for creative tacos, burritos, and house-made salsas. The menu features fusion flavors alongside traditional Mexican favorites. The small, vibrant space with outdoor seating has a loyal local following. Named one of Daytona's best cheap eats.
groupDiningNotes: Small restaurant - groups of up to 10. Outdoor patio helps with overflow. Counter service is quick for groups.

name: Angell & Phelps Chocolate Factory
address: 154 S Beach St, Daytona Beach, FL 32114
description: Angell & Phelps has been making hand-dipped chocolates in Daytona Beach since 1925. The factory and shop offer free tours showing how their famous chocolates, turtles, and fudge are made. The ice cream parlor and candy shop are perfect for a sweet stop. One of Daytona's oldest and most cherished businesses.
groupDiningNotes: Free factory tours for groups of any size. The shop and cafe can accommodate groups. Group chocolate-making classes available by appointment.

name: Dancing Avocado Kitchen
address: 110 S Beach St, Daytona Beach, FL 32114
description: Dancing Avocado Kitchen is a vegetarian and vegan-friendly cafe in downtown Daytona Beach serving fresh, healthy food with creative flavors. The avocado-focused menu includes wraps, bowls, smoothies, and house-made baked goods. The colorful, funky atmosphere reflects the artistic Beach Street neighborhood. A refreshing option in a beach town dominated by fried seafood.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 12 can be seated together. Counter service is efficient for groups. Vegan and vegetarian options make it inclusive for diverse dietary needs.

name: Third Wave Cafe
address: 204 Flagler Ave, New Smyrna Beach, FL 32169
description: Third Wave Cafe is a trendy New Smyrna Beach restaurant on Flagler Avenue serving creative New American cuisine and craft cocktails. Known for innovative brunch dishes, fresh seafood, and seasonal dinner menus. The modern, airy space with an open kitchen reflects the beach town's growing food scene. A NSB favorite for locals and visitors.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 20 can be seated inside or on the patio. Brunch is popular, so reserve ahead for groups on weekends. Custom group menus available for dinner.

name: Clear Sky Beachside Cafe
description: Clear Sky Beachside Cafe is a Clearwater Beach staple located steps from the famous white sand beach. The menu features fresh seafood, creative cocktails, and Florida-inspired dishes. Outdoor dining on the bustling Mandalay Avenue is the way to go. Known for the fish tacos, ahi tuna nachos, and key lime pie.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 20 on the patio. The central Clearwater Beach location is convenient for groups. Reservations recommended during peak season.

name: Frenchy's Rockaway Grill
description: Frenchy's has been a Clearwater Beach institution since 1981, famous for its super grouper sandwich and fresh Gulf seafood. The original Rockaway Grill sits right on the beach with toes-in-the-sand dining. Live music, tropical cocktails, and stunning Gulf sunsets make it quintessential Florida. Multiple locations on Clearwater Beach.
groupDiningNotes: Beach-side seating for groups of any size. First come, first served, so arrive early for large groups. The casual atmosphere is perfect for group beach days.

name: Columbia Restaurant St. Armands
description: The Columbia Restaurant at St. Armands Circle brings the legendary Tampa Cuban-Spanish dining experience to Sarasota. The same 1905 Salad, paella, and Cuban bread in a beautiful St. Armands setting. The outdoor courtyard is one of the most charming dining spots on Florida's Gulf Coast. Part of the Columbia family since 1959.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining for groups of 15 to 60. The courtyard is ideal for group dinners. Same group-friendly menus as the Tampa flagship.

name: Ceviche Tapas Bar & Restaurant
address: 10 Beach Dr NE, St. Petersburg, FL 33701
description: Ceviche is a vibrant Spanish tapas bar on Beach Drive in downtown St. Petersburg. The extensive tapas menu features over 100 dishes from croquetas to patatas bravas to grilled octopus. Live flamenco music on weekends and a stunning waterfront location make it a St. Pete favorite. The sangria program is one of the best in Florida.
groupDiningNotes: Tapas sharing style is naturally group-friendly. Private dining for up to 50. The paella for groups is a showstopper. Live flamenco adds energy to celebrations.

name: The Mill
description: The Mill is an upscale restaurant in downtown St. Petersburg featuring a chef-driven menu of seasonal New American cuisine. The house-made pastas, fresh seafood, and craft cocktails showcase Florida ingredients with global techniques. The elegant, modern dining room in the historic Birchwood building overlooks the waterfront.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining for groups of 10 to 30. The Birchwood event spaces can accommodate larger parties. Custom tasting menus available.

name: Bier Boutique
description: Bier Boutique is a craft beer-focused gastropub on Central Avenue in St. Pete's Grand Central District. The rotating tap list features 40 craft beers alongside a menu of elevated pub fare including gourmet burgers, duck fat fries, and seasonal small plates. The eclectic, art-filled space reflects St. Pete's creative culture.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 20 at communal tables. The craft beer selection makes it fun for group tastings. Casual, walkable from many downtown hotels.

name: Red Mesa Restaurant
description: Red Mesa is a popular St. Petersburg Mexican and Southwestern restaurant known for its creative tacos, enchiladas, and margaritas. The menu blends traditional Mexican flavors with Southwestern flair. The Red Mesa Cantina on 4th Street and the Mercado at Sundial both draw enthusiastic crowds. A Tampa Bay favorite for Mexican food.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 20 can be accommodated. The sharing-style menu works great for groups. Margarita pitchers available for the table.

name: Gratzzi Italian Grille
description: Gratzzi is a popular Italian restaurant in downtown St. Pete's Sundial center featuring classic Italian dishes, wood-fired pizzas, and an open-air patio. The chicken parmigiana and veal marsala are longtime favorites. Live music and a lively bar scene make it a go-to for locals. The outdoor seating on 2nd Street is ideal for people-watching.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 25 on the patio or inside. The Sundial location is convenient for downtown groups. Italian family-style group menus available.

name: Rumba Island Bar & Grill
address: 1800 Gulf to Bay Blvd, Clearwater, FL 33765
description: Rumba Island Bar brings Caribbean-inspired flavors to Clearwater with a menu of island cuisine, rum cocktails, and tropical seafood. The jerk chicken, coconut shrimp, and rum punch are favorites. The tiki bar atmosphere with island music creates a vacation vibe year-round. A fun escape from the mainland without leaving Clearwater.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 25 can be seated together. The island party atmosphere is great for celebrations. Rum tasting packages available for groups.

name: Bob Heilman's Beachcomber
description: Bob Heilman's Beachcomber has been a Clearwater Beach landmark since 1948, serving prime steaks, fresh seafood, and the famous Back-to-the-Farm Fried Chicken. The old-school atmosphere with white tablecloths and professional service recalls a golden era of dining. The lounge features live music nightly. A true Florida classic.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining for groups of 10 to 50. The vintage atmosphere adds character to group celebrations. One of the oldest restaurants in Clearwater.

name: Kappy's Subs
description: Kappy's Subs is a Maitland institution serving overstuffed sub sandwiches since 1969. The hand-cut deli meats, fresh-baked bread, and generous portions have earned a devoted following for over 50 years. The Italian sub and Reuben are legendary. The small, no-frills deli focuses entirely on sandwich perfection.
groupDiningNotes: Limited seating inside. Groups should consider takeout for picnics at nearby Lake Lily Park. Sub platters available for group orders.

name: Yellow Dog Eats
description: Yellow Dog Eats is a quirky, eclectic BBQ restaurant housed in a 1912 general store in downtown Windermere. The menu features creative sandwiches, smoked meats, and craft cocktails. The vintage decor, live music, and outdoor patio create a unique atmosphere. A beloved local favorite known for the Jamaican Jerk Pork and pulled chicken.
groupDiningNotes: Indoor and patio seating for groups of up to 25. Live music nights are great for group outings. The unique atmosphere makes it a memorable group destination.

name: Hollerbach's Willow Tree Cafe
description: Hollerbach's Willow Tree Cafe is an authentic German restaurant and biergarten in historic downtown Sanford. Schnitzel, bratwurst, spaetzle, and German beers are served in a Bavarian atmosphere with live polka music and communal tables. The biergarten hosts lively events and the German hospitality is genuine. A Central Florida gem.
groupDiningNotes: The biergarten accommodates groups of 50 or more. Communal tables are naturally group-friendly. Beer steins and group toasts are part of the fun. Private events available.

name: DaVinci's Italian Restaurant
description: DaVinci's at Pointe Orlando serves classic Italian cuisine in a warm, family-friendly setting on International Drive. Hand-made pastas, wood-fired pizzas, and traditional Italian entrees are prepared with imported ingredients. The spacious dining room and outdoor patio are convenient for I-Drive visitors and families.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 30 can be seated together. Italian family-style dining available for groups. Convenient Pointe Orlando location with parking.

name: HMF at The Breakers
address: 1 S County Rd, Palm Beach, FL 33480
description: HMF at The Breakers Palm Beach is the iconic resort's modern American restaurant and lounge. Named after the resort's founder Henry Morrison Flagler, the restaurant features farm-to-table cuisine, craft cocktails, and live entertainment. The grand oceanfront setting in one of America's most storied resorts creates an unforgettable experience.
groupDiningNotes: The Breakers offers private dining for groups of 10 to 200 across multiple venues. HMF lounge area for cocktail receptions. Full resort event planning services available.

name: Surfside Diner
description: Surfside Diner is a retro 1950s-style diner in downtown Stuart serving classic American breakfast and lunch. Milkshakes, burgers, pancakes, and omelets in a vintage chrome-and-neon setting. The friendly, small-town atmosphere reflects Stuart's charm. A fun stop for families and anyone who loves old-school diners.
groupDiningNotes: Booths and counter seating for groups of up to 10. The nostalgic atmosphere is fun for group brunches. No reservations - walk-in only.

name: Kilwins Vero Beach
description: Kilwins in Vero Beach is a charming chocolate and ice cream shop on Ocean Drive near the beach. Hand-crafted fudge, artisan chocolates, and original recipe ice cream draw visitors and locals alike. The sweet aromas and colorful displays make it a must-stop on any Vero Beach visit. A family-friendly treat destination.
groupDiningNotes: Small shop - groups browse and order at the counter. Great for a group dessert stop. Custom gift boxes and platters can be ordered for events.

name: Cafe Panache
address: 275 Key Deer Blvd, Big Pine Key, FL 33043
description: Cafe Panache is a hidden gem in the Lower Keys serving French-Caribbean fusion cuisine in a casual island setting. Fresh local seafood meets classic French techniques with Caribbean spices. The intimate restaurant is a favorite among Keys locals who appreciate fine food without the Key West crowds. BYOB adds to the charm.
groupDiningNotes: Small restaurant seating up to 20. Groups of 6 to 8 are comfortable. BYOB is a bonus for group dinners. Reservations required.

name: Guanabanas
description: Guanabanas is a unique waterfront restaurant in Jupiter set under massive banyan trees and tropical gardens along the Intracoastal. The open-air, tiki-style setting serves Caribbean-inspired seafood with live music nightly. The ambiance is unlike any other restaurant in South Florida - imagine dining in a tropical jungle. A must-visit destination dining experience.
groupDiningNotes: The sprawling outdoor space accommodates groups of 30 or more. Live music enhances group celebrations. The unique setting makes it a memorable group dining choice.

name: Stuart Coffee Company
description: Stuart Coffee Company is a locally owned coffee shop in the heart of downtown Stuart. Serving specialty coffee, espresso drinks, and fresh pastries in a cozy, community-focused atmosphere. The beans are locally roasted and the baristas are passionate about their craft. A gathering spot for locals and a charming find for visitors.
groupDiningNotes: Cozy cafe seating for groups of up to 8. Great for casual morning meetups. Limited food menu focused on pastries and light bites.

name: Ocean Sun Brewing
address: 299 N Orlando Ave, Cocoa Beach, FL 32931
description: Ocean Sun Brewing is a craft microbrewery in downtown Cocoa Beach producing small-batch beers with a surf culture vibe. The taproom features rotating craft selections, food trucks, and a laid-back atmosphere. A popular gathering spot for locals after beach days and surf sessions.
groupDiningNotes: Taproom accommodates groups of 20 or more. Food trucks rotate daily. Great for casual group hangouts.

name: Canyon Ranch
address: 2831 E Sunrise Blvd, Fort Lauderdale, FL 33304
description: Canyon Ranch in Fort Lauderdale combines a world-class wellness spa with healthy, chef-prepared cuisine. The restaurant focuses on nutrient-dense, flavorful dishes using organic and locally sourced ingredients. Guests enjoy meals as part of the wellness experience. A unique Fort Lauderdale dining option that nourishes body and soul.
groupDiningNotes: Wellness group packages include dining for 10 to 30. Spa and dining experiences can be combined. Reservations required as part of wellness programs.

name: Urbain 40
description: Urbain 40 is an American brasserie on Restaurant Row in Orlando inspired by 1940s supper clubs. The menu features classic steaks, seafood, and Italian dishes in a glamorous Art Deco-styled dining room. Live jazz and cocktails add to the vintage atmosphere. A stylish option among the Restaurant Row dining scene.
groupDiningNotes: Private dining for groups of 12 to 50. The vintage supper club atmosphere enhances celebrations. Live entertainment adds energy to group events.

name: Alto Mare Fish Bar
description: Alto Mare Fish Bar is a seafood-focused restaurant in Tampa's Hyde Park neighborhood serving Italian-influenced coastal cuisine. Fresh oysters, crudo, whole-roasted fish, and house-made pastas highlight the menu. The chic, modern interior with marble bar creates an upscale casual vibe. One of Tampa's newer culinary additions.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 16 at communal tables. The raw bar and shared plates work well for groups. Reservations recommended.

name: Daily Eats
description: Daily Eats is a popular SoHo breakfast and lunch spot in Tampa serving creative twists on comfort food. The short rib grilled cheese, fried chicken biscuit, and tater tot poutine have a cult following. The casual, diner-style atmosphere keeps the focus on good food. Lines form on weekend mornings.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 10 can be seated together. Arrive early on weekends to avoid long waits. Counter service is quick.

name: Bartaco
description: Bartaco in Tampa's Hyde Park Village serves globally-inspired street tacos and craft cocktails in a beach-casual setting. The menu draws from Mexican, Thai, and Mediterranean flavors with tacos priced individually for mix-and-match dining. Frozen cocktails and the breezy patio create a laidback vibe.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 20 on the patio. The taco sharing format is ideal for groups. Cocktail pitchers available. Walk-in only - no reservations.

name: Gaspar's Grotto
description: Gaspar's Grotto is a pirate-themed bar and restaurant in Ybor City named after the legendary pirate Jose Gaspar. The fun, kitschy atmosphere features pirate decor, live music, and a massive outdoor patio. Burgers, wings, and bar food fuel the party. Especially popular during Gasparilla festivities.
groupDiningNotes: Large outdoor patio accommodates groups of any size. The festive pirate theme is great for group celebrations. Live music nightly.

name: The Coop
description: The Coop is a Southern comfort food restaurant in Hyde Park Village specializing in fried chicken, biscuits, and classic sides. The buttermilk fried chicken, chicken and waffles, and mac and cheese are standouts. The farmhouse-chic decor and fast-casual service make it a quick, satisfying meal option.
groupDiningNotes: Counter service with ample seating for groups. Family-style platters available. Great for casual group lunches.

name: Perry's Ocean Edge Resort Restaurant
address: 2209 S Atlantic Ave, Daytona Beach, FL 32118
description: Perry's Ocean Edge Resort features an oceanfront restaurant serving fresh seafood and American classics with direct Atlantic Ocean views. The outdoor pool deck dining and tiki bar add a resort feel. Known for the seafood buffet and Sunday brunch. A Daytona Beach Shores institution since the 1940s.
groupDiningNotes: Group seating for up to 40 in the dining room. Pool deck and tiki bar area for casual group gatherings. Resort event spaces for larger functions.

name: Bosphorus Turkish Cuisine
description: Bosphorus brings authentic Turkish cuisine to Orlando's Restaurant Row with kebabs, mezze, and house-made pide bread baked in a stone oven. The lamb dishes, hummus, and baklava are exceptional. The elegant dining room features Turkish decor and warm hospitality. One of the few authentic Turkish restaurants in Central Florida.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 25 can be seated together. The mezze sharing style is ideal for groups. Belly dancing entertainment on select nights.

name: Beach Place Guesthouse Grill
address: 1445 S Atlantic Ave, Cocoa Beach, FL 32931
description: Beach Place Guesthouse Grill offers casual oceanfront dining on Cocoa Beach with fresh seafood, burgers, and tropical cocktails. The open-air setting directly on the beach features tiki torches and sand underfoot. Live music on weekends and a friendly, no-shoes-required vibe make it a true beach dining experience.
groupDiningNotes: Open-air beach seating for groups of any size. The casual beach atmosphere is perfect for group gatherings. No reservations needed.

name: Embargo Kitchen & Bar
description: Embargo Kitchen & Bar in Thornton Park serves Latin-Caribbean inspired cuisine with creative cocktails in a vibrant atmosphere. The menu features ceviche, empanadas, and Caribbean-spiced entrees. The lively bar scene and colorful decor make it a popular happy hour and dinner destination in one of Orlando's trendiest neighborhoods.
groupDiningNotes: Groups of up to 20 on the patio. Cocktail packages and group menus available. The festive atmosphere is ideal for celebrations.
